title: Aureline settings lock-state reason packet
description: Boundary schema for the user-visible lock-state reason grammar used by settings inspectors, write previews, import and migration review, optional sync, support export, and downgraded reads. The setting-definition registry remains the schema truth; this packet explains why a resolved value or proposed write is inherited, locked, blocked, downgraded, alias-only, or read-only without requiring implementation logs. Raw secrets, raw paths, raw hostnames, raw tenant identifiers, and raw source content never appear in this packet.

settings_precedence_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the precedence, lock-state, and write-scope grammar.

opaque_ref.description: Opaque ref safe for UI, CLI, support export, audit, and fixtures. Do not place raw paths, URLs, hostnames, tokens, tenant ids, or source text here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short prose safe for review surfaces and support exports. Must not contain raw secrets, raw URLs, raw hostnames, raw paths, raw command lines, raw logs, or raw source content.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp rendered as an RFC 3339 string at the schema boundary.

setting_id.description: Canonical stable dotted lower-snake setting id after alias redirection.

resolution_scope.description: User-visible precedence scope. These scopes map onto the lower-level settings ADR scopes but preserve package, template, environment, remote-target, policy, and emergency distinctions for inspectors.

lock_state_class.description: User-visible lock posture. This is explanation vocabulary, not the low-level effective_setting.lock_state enum.

lock_reason_code.description: Frozen reason-code vocabulary. Codes explain why a setting row or write target is inherited, locked, blocked, downgraded, read-only, or alias-only.

reason_effect.description: How this reason affects read or write behavior.

repair_action.description: Stable repair-action vocabulary. Surfaces may render product copy for these actions but may not invent new hidden repair classes.

fixture_metadata.description: Checked-in fixture metadata. Production emitters omit this field.

authority_ref.description: Optional owner or authority that created the reason, such as a policy bundle, package, template lineage, device registry row, remote target, or migration row.

blocks_write.description: True when this reason independently blocks the proposed write to the named scope.

expires_at.description: Expiry for stale, emergency, approval, or degraded read-only reasons; null when not bounded.

support_export_safe.description: False only when even the reason metadata must be omitted from ordinary support exports.

lock_state_reason.description: One lock-state reason row. Reason stacks preserve all rows instead of flattening them into a single disabled-state string.

effective_blocks_write.description: True when at least one active reason blocks the proposed write or when the resolved read is read-only.

reason_stack.description: Ordered reasons, most decisive first. Surfaces render this stack instead of scraping logs.
